name: Ancient Writing

options.label: [Elegance]
options.description: Remove a card from your deck.

options.label: [Simplicity]
effects: All of the player's basic Strikes and basic Defends are  Upgraded.

raw_options:
[Elegance] Remove a card from your deck.


[Simplicity] Upgrade all Strikes and Defends.
• All of the player's basic Strikes and basic Defends are  Upgraded.
raw_dialogue:
Scaling the city, you notice a wall covered in the writing of Ancients. As you try to wrap your head around what the puzzling symbols and glyphs could mean, the writing begins to glow.
Suddenly, the message becomes clear...


[Elegance]

The answer was elegance.
Of course.


[Simplicity]

The truth is always simple.

name: Augmenter

options.label: [Test J.A.X.]
effects: Adds  J.A.X. to the player's deck.

options.label: [Become Test Subject]
effects: Read more on Transform rules here.

options.label: [Ingest Mutagens]
effects: Rewards the player with  Mutagenic Strength.

raw_options:
[Test J.A.X.] Get JAXXED.
• Adds  J.A.X. to the player's deck.
[Become Test Subject] Transform 2 cards.
• Read more on Transform rules here.
[Ingest Mutagens]  Obtain a special relic.
• Rewards the player with  Mutagenic Strength.
raw_dialogue:
A man with an eyepatch and a devilish grin strides up to you.

"Hey there, stranger. Interested in advancing science? I can make you stronger than any training or blessing. You're gonna need it if you're one of those heroes with a death wish."

"Whad'ya say?"

[Test J.A.X.]

"Excellent."

The man hands over a dangerous looking syringe filled with a glowing liquid before skulking off into a shadowy alleyway.


[Become Test Subject]

"Marvelous."

You quaff the mysterious substance. Immediately, you are invigorated and feel your muscle fibers twitch.


[Ingest Mutagens]

"Superb."

The man injects you with three unknown substances and pulls out a notepad. As you begin to feel light-headed, he starts to frantically write down notes.

Losing track of time completely, by the time you regain your senses, the shady character has disappeared.

name: Council of Ghosts

options.label: [Accept]
options.description: Receive 5 (A15+: 3) Apparitions. Lose X Max HP.
effects: X is equal to 50% of the player's Max HP, rounded up.

options.label: [Refuse]
effects: You are then given the option to

raw_options:
[Accept] Receive 5 (A15+: 3) Apparitions. Lose X Max HP.
• X is equal to 50% of the player's Max HP, rounded up.
[Refuse] Nothing happens.


You are then given the option to [Leave].
raw_dialogue:
As you continue your ascent, thick black smoke begins to billow out of the ground and walls around you, coalescing into three masked forms that start to speak.

"Another puppet of Neow I think."
"AGREED! SHE ALWAYS MAKES THE FUNNEST TOYS!"

You notice an over-sized grin as the third addresses you.
"Ignore the others... Would you like a taste of our power?"
[Accept]

"Excellent!"
As the ghostly shape speaks, you notice its large mouth opening wider and wider. Thick black smoke spews forth and envelops the room. You cannot see or breathe...

Just before you lose consciousness, the sensation stops.

Whatever those things were, they are gone now. You continue on, feeling rather hollow.


[Refuse]

"How disappointing..."
"You will join us sooner or later."
"HA HA HA HAHAHA!"
The shapes fade away, leaving only the unnerving laughter ringing in your ears.

name: Cursed Tome

options.label: [Read]
options.description: Begin reading the tome (starts the event sequence).

options.label: [Take]
effects: Receive one random book relic: Enchiridion, Nilry's Codex, or Necronomicon.
Player doesn't know which book until after choosing [Take].
Total HP cost if committed: 16 (A15+: 21) HP (1+2+3+10/15)

options.label: [Stop]
options.description: Lose 3 HP. Give up on the book.
effects: Exit without obtaining the book relic.

notes: This is a SEQUENTIAL event: [Read] → [Continue] x3 → [Take]/[Stop]. Player can [Leave] only at the start.
Tungsten Rod reduces total HP lost by 4 (to 12/17) if player takes the book, or to 5 if player stops.
Practical summary: Either commit to 16 (A15+: 21) HP for random book relic, or [Leave] immediately for 0 cost.
raw_options:
[Read]
[Continue] Lose 1 HP.


[Continue] Lose 2 HP.


[Continue] Lose 3 HP.


[Take] Obtain the book. Lose 10 (A15+: 15) HP.

The book may be  Enchiridion,  Nilry's Codex, or  Necronomicon. The player does not know which book they will get until after they choose [Take] and lose their HP.

[Stop] Lose 3 HP.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
Practical Options
Because the player can choose to leave the event immediately and lose 0 HP instead of selecting [Continue] 3 times and then backing out with [Stop] while losing a total of 9 HP, there are really only 2 practical ways to navigate the event. The player can either commit to all the HP loss and obtain the book, or leave immediately to do nothing.
For simplicity, the event's options can be thought of in this way instead:
• [Read], then all 3 [Continue], and finally [Take] Obtain the book. Lose 16 (A15+: 21) HP.
• [Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
In an abandoned temple, you find a giant book, open, riddled with cryptic writings. 
As you try to interpret the elaborate script, it begins to shift and morph into writing you are familiar with.


[Read]

Odd. The book seems to be about an Ancient named Neow. 
This piques your interest, but you have a general feeling of malaise.

[Continue]

The Ancient of Resurrection, Neow, was exiled to the bottom of the Spire. 
You feel compelled to read more, but your body begins to ache.

[Continue]

Seeking vengeance, Neow blesses outsiders, using them for her own purposes. 
You are starting to feel very weak and tired...

[Continue]

Those resurrected by Neow remember only fragments of their past selves, cursed to fight for eternity. 
As you near the final page, your old wounds begin to reopen!

[Take]

Upon finishing the tome, you decide to take it with you. With proof in hand, will you retain your memories?


[Stop]

With incredible strain and willpower, you resist the trance of the tome and SLAM it shut. 
You turn and exit the temple, feeling drained...






[Leave]

You exit, feeling a dark energy emanating from the book on the pedestal.

name: Designer In-Spire

options.label: [Adjustments]
options.description: Lose 40 (A15+: 50) Gold. One of the following rewards appears.
effects: Upgrade a card.
Upgrade two random cards.

options.label: [Clean Up]
options.description: Lose 60 (A15+: 75) Gold. One of the following rewards appears.
effects: Remove a card.
Transform two cards.

options.label: [Full Service]
options.description: Lose 90 (A15+: 110) Gold.  Remove a card, then upgrade a random card.

raw_options:
[Adjustments] Lose 40 (A15+: 50) Gold. One of the following rewards appears.
• Upgrade a card.
• Upgrade two random cards.
[Clean Up] Lose 60 (A15+: 75) Gold. One of the following rewards appears.
• Remove a card.
• Transform two cards.
[Full Service] Lose 90 (A15+: 110) Gold.  Remove a card, then upgrade a random card.


[Punch] Lose 3 (A15+: 5) HP.
raw_dialogue:
You discover a colorful shop with the banner "IN-SPIRE" and walk in to see what's inside.
"No, no way. Nope. Can't let you in!" 
A man with ridiculous clothing appears at the entrance to bar you.

[Continue]



"This will not do, no no. What is this style? Disgusting! Are you bleeeeding? Groooss. Business?? You a customer? Fine. Whaaatever." 
He lets out an exaggerated sigh and points at a list of services. 
The services seem fine, but you would rather punch this smug man in his smug face.

[Adjustments], [Clean Up], and [Full Service]

"Okay, bye bye now." 
...should've punched him.


[Punch]

You punch him so hard your fist hurts. 
"My FACE!! Now I'll have to-" 
He fainted. Who's groooss and bleeeeding now?

name: Forgotten Altar

options.label: [Offer: Golden Idol]
options.description: Obtain a special Relic (Bloody Idol). Lose Golden Idol.
effects: Receive Bloody Idol relic
Golden Idol is consumed

options.label: [Sacrifice]
options.description: Gain 5 Max HP. Lose X HP.
effects: X is equal to 25% (A15+: 35%) of the player's Max HP.

notes: Golden Idol is only obtained from the  Golden Idol Event.
If the  Golden Idol is turned into the  Bloody Idol,  The Moai Head Event in Act 3 will no longer accept it as an offering.
raw_options:
[Offer: Golden Idol] Obtain a special  Relic. Lose  Golden Idol.
• If the player does not have  Golden Idol, this option is replaced with [Locked] Requires: Golden Idol.
[Sacrifice] Gain 5 Max HP. Lose X HP.
• X is equal to 25% (A15+: 35%) of the player's Max HP.
[Desecrate] Become Cursed - Decay.
raw_dialogue:
In front of you sits an altar to a forgotten god.

Atop the altar sits an ornate female statue with arms outstretched.

She calls out to you, demanding sacrifice.

[Offer: Golden Idol]

As you gently set the idol onto the altar a cold wind swirls throughout the room.

The arms of the statue begin to discolor and crumble.

Your golden idol begins to dull in color and begins bleeding from its eyes.

The bleeding never ceases.


[Sacrifice]

You stand on the altar and cut your wrists.

As the blood spills out in sacrifice, the arms of the statue reach out and close around your eyes.

Everything goes dark.

You wake up a short time later feeling a new potential surging through you.


[Desecrate]

You lash out and smash the statue in front of you, breaking the magical hold the room had placed upon you.

A dark wail echoes all around you, and you can feel the cursed magic seep into your bones.

name: Knowing Skull

loop_mechanic.description: Event repeats until [How do I leave?] is chosen

options.label: [A Pick Me Up?]
options.description: Get a Potion. Lose X HP.
notes: Works even with full potion slots or Sozu - still pay HP cost

options.label: [Success?]
options.description: Get a Colorless card. Lose X HP.

notes: Bloody Idol can greatly increase the number of times the player can choose [Riches?] since they will heal 5 HP every time it gives them Gold.
Tungsten Rod will reduce the HP lost by 1 every time an option is selected.
raw_options:
The following options are presented to the player until they decide to leave the event. X is equal to 10% of the player's Max HP (minimum of 6 HP) plus the number of times they have chosen that specific option (Lose 6 HP, then Lose 7 HP, then Lose 8 HP, etc.)


[A Pick Me Up?] Get a Potion. Lose X HP.
• A random Potion is immediately added to the player's left-most empty potion slot.
• The player is able to select this option even if all their potion slots are full or if they possess  Sozu. They still lose HP and this option's HP loss is still increased by 1 even though no potion is obtained.
[Riches?] Gain 90 Gold. Lose X HP.


[Success?] Get a Colorless card. Lose X HP.
• A random Uncommon Colorless card is added to the player's deck.
[How do I leave?] Lose X HP.
• This event repeats until this option is chosen.
raw_dialogue:
You find yourself in an old, decorated chamber. In the center of the room, a large skull sits atop an ornate pedestal. As you approach, the skull bursts into flames and turns to face you.


[Continue]


"WHAT IS IT YOU SEEK? WHAT IS IT YOU OFFER?" 
In sync with its final words, the door behind you slams shut.

[A Pick Me Up?]

"PERHAPS THIS WILL HELP?" 
You obtain a card. 
"ANYTHING ELSE?"

The player is able to choose any of the Options again. [A Pick Me Up?] now costs 1 more HP than it did previously.



[Riches?]

"DRINK UP!" 
You obtain a potion. 
"ANYTHING ELSE?"

The player is able to choose any of the Options again. [Riches?] now costs 1 more HP than it did previously.


[Success?]

"YOU MORTALS NEVER CHANGE. IT IS DONE." 
Gold rains down on you! 
"ANYTHING ELSE?"

The player is able to choose any of the Options again. [Success?] now costs 1 more HP than it did previously.


[How do I leave?]

"BEHIND YOU, MORTAL." 
You peek behind the skull. Surely enough, there is a door.

The event ends, and the player is given the option to [Leave].

name: Masked Bandits

options.label: [Fight!]
effects: Starts a combat against the three bandits ( Pointy,  Romeo, and  Bear). Upon winning, obtain regular card rewards, 25 - 35 Gold, and  Red Mask.

notes: Although the fight can be quite dangerous and offers a Relic reward, it does not count as an Elite combat for the sake of  Preserved Insect,  Sling of Courage,  Black Star, and  Slaver's Collar.
If the player has  Red Mask, the Act 3 Event  Tomb of Lord Red Mask will have an option to gain 222 Gold for free.
raw_options:
[Pay] Lose ALL of your Gold.


[Fight!]
• Starts a combat against the three bandits ( Pointy,  Romeo, and  Bear). Upon winning, obtain regular card rewards, 25 - 35 Gold, and  Red Mask.
raw_dialogue:
You encounter a group of bandits wearing large red masks.

"Hello, pay up to pass... a reasonable fee of ALL your gold will do! Heh heh!"

[Pay]

Hehehe.. Thanks for the gold!

Oh, I love gold. It's so nice.

shiny shiny chits they are!

[Continue]

Hey Bear, hey!

This guy gave us all his gold! What a sucker, right?

Get this, I just had to ask nicely. Who knew?!

I certainly didn't! What a chump!


[Continue]

Gang, let's all have a laugh for this wondrous occasion!

Hahaah

Ho HOH hoho!

Hoh!


[Leave]


If the player returns from the Map screen instead of progressing to the next floor, the event has extra dialogue:

Oh? You're still here?

Did you overhear something? Didn't think so.

*snerk* ...loser.... Hahaha haaah


[Fight!]


(No dialogue; the player enters combat with the Bandits)

name: N'loth

options.label: [Offer: Relic A]
options.description: Lose this relic. Obtain a special relic.

options.label: [Offer: Relic B]
options.description: Lose this relic. Obtain a special relic.
effects: Rewards the player with  N'loth's Gift.

notes: N'loth can pick Relics that activate their effects only upon being picked up, as well as used-up Relics.
For example, offering Relics like  Mango or  Old Coin will not remove the max HP or Gold granted by them, effectively removing the downside of  N'loth's Gift.
raw_options:
Note: "Relic A" and "Relic B" will be randomly chosen from the player's currently held Relics.

[Offer: Relic A] Lose this relic. Obtain a special relic.
[Offer: Relic B] Lose this relic. Obtain a special relic.
• Rewards the player with  N'loth's Gift.
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
An odd creature with a hunched back sprouting several tentacles is scrounging through a pile of trash and debris in front of you. As you approach, he shuffles towards you in a non-threatening manner.

"N'loth hungry. Feed N'loth."


[Offer: Relic A] or [Offer: Relic B]

 Holding the relic out towards him, N'loth snatches it out of your hand with his tentacles, dislocates his jaw, and slurps down your offer in one quick gulp.

He gives you a large, toothy grin as more tentacles appear from behind his cloak, these ones brandishing an impossibly neat looking box. He pushes it towards you until you take it.


[Leave]

You shake your head. N'loth hunches even further and sighs, then scuttles away.

name: Old Beggar

options.label: [Offer Gold]
options.description: 75 Gold: Remove a card from your deck.

raw_options:
[Offer Gold] 75 Gold: Remove a card from your deck. 


[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
An old beggar cloaked in fur reaches his hands out towards you as you pass. "Spare some coin, child?"

[Offer Gold]

The beggar takes off its cloak to reveal that he is Cleric! 
"You are a kind soul. Receive my purification!" he screams. 
You are unsure if he is grateful or mad.

[Leave]


If the player returns from the Map screen instead of progressing to the next floor, the event has extra dialogue:

"I hope you do better this time, friend!" he shouts.

Wondering what was implied by this, you push forward.


[Leave]

The beggar looks to the floor as you pass. 
"You will never make a difference... You never do."

name: Pleading Vagrant

options.label: [Offer Gold]
conditions: If the player does not have enough Gold, this option will be greyed out.

options.label: [Rob]
options.description: Obtain a Relic. Become Cursed -  Shame.

notes: Omamori can be used to prevent the  Shame from [Rob] if it has any charges remaining.
raw_options:
[Offer Gold] 85 Gold: Obtain a Relic.
• If the player does not have enough Gold, this option will be greyed out.
[Rob] Obtain a Relic. Become Cursed -  Shame.


[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
While sneaking past a group of shrouded figures, one of them approaches you.

"Got anything for me friend? Please... maybe some Coin?"

"I just need somewhere to stay, I have treasures I can trade..."

He seems delusional, but harmless.


[Offer Gold]

"Oh yes, yes! Here here, a fair trade!"


[Rob]

You snatch the precious relic from his clutches and walk away.

From behind you hear,

"Have you no shame? HAVE YOU NO SHAAAAAME?!"

You have some shame.


[Leave]


(No dialogue)

name: The Colosseum

options.label: [Fight]
options.description: Fight Blue Slaver and Red Slaver (no rewards).
effects: Mandatory fight - no escape option.
End-of-combat effects (Burning Blood, Self Repair) still trigger.
Smoke Bomb can be used to flee but still proceeds to Phase 2.

options.label: [COWARDICE]
effects: Event ends with no additional rewards.

options.label: [VICTORY]
options.description: Fight Taskmaster and Gremlin Nob for massive rewards.
effects: Rewards: 100 Gold, 1 Rare Relic, 1 Uncommon Relic, card reward, possible Potion.
Treated as Elite combat (Preserved Insect, Sling of Courage, Slaver's Collar work).
Black Star does NOT double relics (always exactly 1 Rare + 1 Uncommon).

notes: This is a TWO-PHASE event: Phase 1 (mandatory fight) → Phase 2 (escape or glory).
Both fights can trigger Potion drop roll, but Phase 1 potion is never shown.
Phase 2 [VICTORY] fight is one of the best reward opportunities in Act 2.
raw_options:
[Fight]
• The player enters a fight with a  Blue Slaver and  Red Slaver. This fight gives no rewards.
After the fight ends, a choice has to be made: 
[COWARDICE] Escape.


[VICTORY] A powerful fight with many rewards.

The player enters a fight with  Taskmaster and  Gremlin Nob.
The rewards are 100 Gold, 1 Rare Relic, 1 Uncommon Relic, a card reward, and sometimes a Potion.
raw_dialogue:
Thwack!!! 
.. 
...... 
... 
You were knocked unconscious...
 
[Continue]


Groggy and with a throbbing head, you awaken to find yourself thrown in the center of a massive stadium with an overflowing audience of Slavers, Cultists, and other denizens of the City! 
An armored giant with a golden crown bellows at you from atop, 
"WE NOW BEGIN THE 4200TH COMBAT!!!!" 
A gate on the opposite side opens...

[Fight]


(The player enters combat with a  Blue Slaver and  Red Slaver. After ending the fight, more dialogue is presented.)


"WELL DONE, WEAKLING!" 
The giant mock claps whilst he riles up the crowd with exaggerated gestures. 
Gold and confetti shower you! 
"TIME FOR THE REAL CHALLENGE!!" 

The last battle left a small hole in the Colosseum's wall, you can easily escape through there while everyone is distracted. 
Do you stay and fight?


[COWARDICE]


(No dialogue)


[VICTORY]


(No dialogue, the player enters combat with  Taskmaster and  Gremlin Nob)

name: The Joust

options.label: [Owner]
effects: There is no option to avoid betting.

notes: If the player possesses  Ectoplasm, this event is a mandatory loss of 50 Gold. There is no option to leave the event without betting, and any potential winnings cannot be gained.
The player may consider using the expected value (EV) of each option to determine who they want to bet on.
The EV of betting on the Murderer is a net increase of 20 Gold (a 70% chance to receive 100 Gold is an expected 70 Gold, minus the 50 Gold that was bet. 0.7 x 100 - 50 = 20)
The EV of betting on the Owner is a net increase of 25 Gold (a 30% chance to receive 250 Gold is an expected 75 Gold, minus the 50 Gold that was bet. 0.3 x 250 - 50 = 25)

It may be more beneficial to ignore the EV and instead consider whether the relatively safe 50 extra Gold by betting on the Murderer is more useful to the player's current run compared to risking the bet on the Owner and likely losing their 50 Gold for the more unlikely net gain of 200 Gold.
raw_options:
[Murderer] Bet 50 Gold - 70%: win 100 Gold.
[Owner] Bet 50 Gold - 30%: win 250 Gold.
There is no option to avoid betting.
raw_dialogue:
As you make your way through the large buildings you come across a long narrow bridge and spot knights on either side, facing one another. 
You approach... 
"HALT!" 
A knight forcefully gestures you to stop with its giant lance.

[Continue]

"Today is the day I must settle the score with the murderer of my beloved pet, Noodles. Until then, you may not pass. 
Fellow witness, why don't you bet on who you think will emerge victorious?"

[Murderer]

"I can't believe you're betting against Noodles!" 
Furious, he clamps down his helmet and rushes towards his nemesis.


[Owner]

"Give me strength, Noodles!" 
Clamping down his helmet, the knight charges forward.


*CRASH!!!* *KLAAAANG!* 
*POW!*



Depending on who loses the joust: The owner died. / The nemesis was slain.


When losing the bet:
You lost the bet, but at least you weren't gouged by a lance.
When winning the bet:
You win the bet. Unsure what to think, you grab your winnings and leave.

name: The Library

options.label: [Read]
options.description: Choose 1 of 20 cards to add to your deck.

options.label: [Sleep]
effects: X is equal to 33% (A15+: 20%) of the player's Max HP.

notes: [Sleep] is unaffected by  Regal Pillow, does not trigger  Dream Catcher, and can still be chosen with  Coffee Dripper.
Mark of the Bloom makes [Sleep] serve only as a way to skip adding a card to your deck as it will no longer heal you.
N'loth's Gift increases the likelihood of each card offered by [Read] being Rare.
raw_options:
[Read] Choose 1 of 20 cards to add to your deck.


[Sleep] Heal X HP.
• X is equal to 33% (A15+: 20%) of the player's Max HP.
raw_dialogue:
You come across an ornate building which appears abandoned. 
A plaque that has been torn free from a wall is on the floor. It reads, "THE LIBRARY". 
Inside, you find countless rows of scrolls, manuscripts, and books. 
You pick one and cozy yourself into a chair for some quiet time.

[Read]
There are 3 possible stories that the player can read. One of the following is chosen at random.

The story is about an insect-controlling teenage girl who aspires to become a hero. The book is filled with creative uses of power, combat strategies, and varying perspectives.
Satisfying.


The story is about a man who journeyed beyond the stars and found himself stuck on a desolate foreign planet. Ingenuity, luck, perseverance, and humor to retain his sanity were his tools to return home.
Fascinating.


The story takes place in a giant isolated building underground as the outside conditions have become unbearable. The novel is mired with conspiracies, propaganda, and injustice. You ponder if similar dynamics are at play within the Spire.
Unsettling.


[Sleep]

Reading is for chumps. 
You doze off in a comfy chair instead. 
Zzz... zzz... ..Zz... 
You wake up feeling refreshed.

name: The Mausoleum

options.label: [Open Coffin]
options.description: Obtain a Relic. 50% (A15+: 100%): Become Cursed -  Writhe

notes: If the player receives  Omamori as the Relic reward, it will not prevent the  Writhe from being gained.
If the player receives  Darkstone Periapt as the Relic reward, it will grant the +6 Max HP.
raw_options:
[Open Coffin] Obtain a Relic. 50% (A15+: 100%): Become Cursed -  Writhe
[Leave] Nothing happens.
raw_dialogue:
Venturing through a series of tombs, you are faced with a large sarcophagus studded with gems in the center of a circular room. 
You cannot make out the writing on the coffin, however, you do notice black fog seeping out from the sides.

[Open Coffin]
The dialogue changes depending on whether the player becomes cursed with  Writhe or not.
If the player does not receive  Writhe:



You push open the coffin. The fog dissipates harmlessly. Inside, you find the mortal remains of a decorated soldier grasping an old relic. You pilfer it and move on.



If the player receives  Writhe:



You push open the coffin. As you do, black fog spews forth and covers the entire room! Inside, you find no body, only a relic. You take it and move onwards, coughing violently.



[Leave]

You continue on your way, leaving the forgotten dead undisturbed.

name: The Nest
raw_options:
[Smash and Grab] Obtain 99 (A15+: 50) Gold.


[Stay in Line] Obtain  Ritual Dagger. Lose 6 HP.
raw_dialogue:
A long line of hooded figures can be seen entering an unassuming cathedral.


Naturally, you join the line and are quickly surrounded by Cultists! 
They ignore you as they gleefully chant and wave their weapons around. 
"MURDER!! MURDER MURDER!!" 
"CAW CAW CAAAAAWWW!" 
"MURDER! MURDER MURDER!!" 
"CAAW CAW CAAAAAAWW!!" 
You eye a Donation Box...

[Smash and Grab]

They didn't even notice.


[Stay in Line]

You decide to stay in line (out of fear) to see what will happen. 
Eventually, you are face-to-face with the leader. A well-dressed cultist hands you an Ornate Dagger. Like the others before you, you slash your forearm and let the blood drip into a misshapen bowl. 
The cultists chant and holler for you! 
"CAAW CAW CAAAAAAWW!!" 
You chant, too. Why not?

name: Vampires

options.label: [Offer: Blood Vial]
options.description: Lose  Blood Vial.  Remove all  Strikes. Receive 5  Bites.
conditions: Only present if the player has  Blood Vial.

options.label: [Accept]
options.description: Remove all  Strikes. Receive 5  Bites. Lose X Max HP.
effects: X is equal to 30% of the player's max HP.

options.label: [Refuse]
effects: You are then given the option to

raw_options:
[Offer: Blood Vial] Lose  Blood Vial.  Remove all  Strikes. Receive 5  Bites.
• Only present if the player has  Blood Vial.
[Accept] Remove all  Strikes. Receive 5  Bites. Lose X Max HP.
• X is equal to 30% of the player's max HP.
[Refuse] Nothing happens.
You are then given the option to [Leave].
raw_dialogue:
Navigating an unlit street, you come across several hooded figures in the midst of some dark ritual. As you approach, they turn to you in eerie unison. The tallest among them bares fanged teeth and extends a long, pale hand towards you.

"Join us brother, and feel the warmth of the Spire."

[Offer: Blood Vial]

The pale figures gasp as you take out the Blood Vial.

"The master's blood... the master's blood! THE MASTER'S BLOOD!"

They all chant fervently as the tall one bows before you. "Drink from His blood, and become one with Him."

The chant growing louder, you consume the contents of the vial. Your vision immediately warps and fades to darkness.


You wake up some time later, alone. An intense hunger passes through your belly. You must feed...


[Accept]

The tall figure grabs your arm, pulls you forward, and sinks his fangs into your neck. You feel a dark force pour into your neck and course through your body.

...

You wake up some time later, alone. An intense hunger passes through your belly. You must feed...


[Refuse]

You step back and raise your weapon in defiance. The tall figure sighs.

"Very well." The entire group of hooded figures morph into a thick black fog that flows away from you.

You are alone once more.
